note: Correctness fix only, per SLM-237's own acceptance criteria: no OOD quality comparison or checkpoint-promotion claim is made from this patch. RSC-A02 (whether the final depth belongs in the auxiliary term) is explicitly out of scope.

verified_defect.historical_formula: sum_d(L_d) / sum_d(w_d)  (an unweighted mean -- w_d bound in the loop but never multiplied)
verified_defect.checked_fixture_evidence: docs/design/iter-slm138-recursive-denoiser-20260720.md recorded recursive_depth_supervision_loss=33.3280029296875 for weights=(0.5, 1.0), and its own recursive_depth_loss_0=26.03812026977539 / recursive_depth_loss_1=23.953886032104492 satisfy (L0+L1)/1.5 = 33.32800... exactly -- the historical defective (unweighted mean) formula, not the intended weighted one. Re-running the fixture after this patch (same weights) now yields the corrected weighted value instead.

before_fix.reproduced_against: actual pre-patch src/slm_training/models/twotower.py (git stash to HEAD, not a hand re-implementation)

mode4_negative_weight_not_rejected.note: The historical loop never multiplies by w at all (the verified defect); a negative weight is accepted unchecked and silently changes the denominator (here -1+3=2, masquerading as if weights were symmetric (1,1)) instead of raising or anti-supervising L0 as a correctly-weighted formula would.

mode3_all_zero_raises.exception: recursive_depth_supervision_weights=(0.0, 0.0) are all zero. An all-zero configuration would silently disable the objective and its telemetry while still looking wired up; use an empty tuple () to explicitly turn the feature off instead.

mode4_negative_weight_raises.exception: recursive_depth_supervision_weights[0] = -1.0 is negative. Weights must be >= 0; a negative weight would anti-supervise that depth instead of merely down-weighting it.

mode5_length_mismatch_raises.exception: recursive_depth_supervision_weights has length 1 ((1.0,)) but the denoiser produced 2 recursion depth(s). The length must match exactly -- no truncation or padding via min(len(...), len(...)) is performed; fix recursive_steps or the weights tuple so they agree.

mode6_stacked_weights_raise.exception: recursive_depth_supervision_weights is non-empty ((0.5, 1.0)) but denoiser_arch='stacked' does not expose recursive_outputs (e.g. a 'stacked' or HF denoiser, or an un-migrated checkpoint). Deep supervision requires a recursive-outputs-capable architecture (e.g. denoiser_arch='shared_recursive'); leave the tuple empty () to keep the feature off for this architecture.

implementation.backward_compatibility: Empty tuple remains feature-off on every architecture (stacked, HF, shared_recursive, and old checkpoints) with no behavior change; malformed non-empty configs now fail with a named, migration-quality ValueError instead of silently truncating or ignoring.

properties_covered: non-empty weights on stacked denoiser raise
empty tuple valid on every architecture, no auxiliary term
fixture JSON metrics agree with manual weighted-mean calculation
